title: Even at noon Titan sits in permanent dusk: distance and thick haze together leave barely a thousandth of Earth's sunlight at the surface — about as dim as Earth ten minutes after sunset, a golden gloom that never lifts - Space Daily
description: Even at noon Titan sits in permanent dusk: distance and thick haze together leave barely a thousandth of Earth's sunlight at the surface — about as dim as Earth ten minutes after sunset, a golden gloom that never lifts  Space DailySaturn's moon Titan runs the same weather cycle as Earth — clouds, rain, rivers and seas — except the liquid doing all the work is methane, and the bedrock underfoot is water frozen at nearly minus 180 degrees, harder than most stone on Earth  Space DailySaturn’s Largest Moon May Hold the Resources for a Space Colony  SciTechDailyTitan holds hundreds of times more liquid hydrocarbons than every known oil and gas reserve on Earth combined, yet you couldn't light a single drop — its air is nitrogen and methane, with almost no oxygen, so a campfire is physically impossible  Space DailyTitan, the interplanetary gas station - News  Techno-Science
